reviews.review_description: I once bought a ticket for the concert but due to work, I missed the timing. I can't even watch the replay since there isn't any. I can't even get a refund. Money down the drain!
Broo i cant even play that game
I thought it was a music game but!!!!!!!!!!! No it’s a dumb anime game

The only way to watch a concert you need to pay 500 Vark coins which equals $50! For one virtual concert, at a specific time, WE ARE NOT MADE OF MONEY. There is a trial concert but it got boring after the first song.
Ever wanted to be one of those sad, lonely, Tokyo men with glow wands, getting wet over schoolgirls singing, then get this. If your a normal person save yourself the bother lol

Very well designed, and good graphics. Really immersive! However I wish that there was more free content. As a point of improvement, I would change the clap mechanic to be with motion and not by hitting the grip buttons (which is kinda annoying)
You are forced to pay money if you want to use it
Just it sooo bad

You need to have certain interests to like this. Are you interested in Japanese? Do you like anime characters/vtubers? Would you pay money for a virtual concert where the performer has a little interaction with the audience but have no idea what they’re saying?

For me, I can check all of those boxes. There was a free concert in March that would make me decide whether I keep this or not. I attended and had a blast! I really hope they have more free concerts to give people a better taste on what Vark has to offer. Are the tickets expensive? Yeah, a bit. I’m looking at a 3 concert pack for about $120 cad. You can toss bouquets onto the stage but like they’re $5-10 each. But this is also supporting a creator who I assume wrote the songs or at least spent a lot of time practicing. They design how the lights shine like an actual concert. Me personally, I’m willing to pay those ticket prices because I think it’s worth it, but you have to make that decision yourself
need to get a grip on things with silly prices ,come back down to earth

reviews.review_title: How has this made it on to oculus store?
reviews.review_description: Just no.

Okay.
So the app is Free.
But I still feel cheated out of time.

reviews.review_title: One of the greatest VR experience I had
reviews.review_description:
I felt this could be the future of global Events, especially given the recent Covid-19 context.
Too bas the app was removed from global store (but it's understandable since being familiar with Japanese language and culture is probably a big requirements to enjoy this app at the moment).
The new "Anyone can do a live" feature is absolutely awesome.

I am really looking forward to seeing how this app evolution will be. Please keep the good work!

reviews.review_title: Boo this garbage should be removed
reviews.review_description: First this game is plagiarizing the hasu miku game and trying to charge 50.00 a pop for what they call a live streaming concert which is bull crap this title should be removed from the oculus store and the developer fined for copyright violations and illegal mis appropriation of funds this is completely garbage don’t download and the details lie in the store it doesn’t mention in the details that it costs 50.00 u.s per concert even Japanese Americans are not that stupid to buy this

developer_response.body:
Hi dingofoxvrfox.

First of all, we want to make clear that we are not violating any copyrights - We have signed contracts with all the companies and artists for them to appear within the app with their names and appearances.

The reason we charge users to join concerts is because they are performed by actual people on-the-spot. It is much like an actual concert venue, except artists in VARK are defined by their avatar identities, and move and talk through their avatars using motion capture technology. We want to ensure that the performers we invite on to our platform can gain profit from the concerts.

reviews.review_description: To see concerts you have to pay that is bad for a game that is free

I LOVED the experience from the trial concert! I went in thinking wow who would pay for this but now I want to pay for it. Its an animated concert and no its not for everyone but there is nothing that is 100% for all people. This is an experience and a very nice one. Download the app and give the trial a chance.

reviews.review_title: Sucks so bad I hate it sooooo much
reviews.review_description: DUDE THIS SUCKS CREATORS MAKE IT SOCIAL AND BE ABLE TO THROW YOUR OWN CONCERT AND PREFORM IF YOU DID THIS I ACTUALLY WOULDN’T BE AS BORED OUT OF MY MIND AS AT THIS MOMENT
I first saw this game and though it would be good, after 15 of trying to find out how to play I found that you just watch some anime person sing in Japanese.

I thought it was a weird thing.
It’s not really a game it’s just a live stream concert thing.
Why the f**k do I have to pay so much money to watch replays
